query.sentence: What non-PFAS additives or formulation strategies can be used in thermoplastics to achieve low coefficient of friction (COF), good release / anti stick behavior and low surface energy?

properties: low coefficient of friction (COF < 0.2 on typical polymer surfaces)
anti‑stick / release behavior suitable for molding or extrusion
thermal stability up to at least 250 °C (typical thermoplastic processing window)
chemical resistance compatible with common solvents and additives
mechanical compatibility: minimal impact on tensile strength, modulus, and elongation of the base polymer
processability: can be dispersed uniformly in melt or solution without phase separation
constraints: must be free of per‑ and polyfluoroalkyl substances (PFAS)
compatible with common thermoplastic matrices such as PET, PP, PE, PC, ABS, and HDPE
available in laboratory scale quantities (< 10 kg) or as a commercial additive
non‑toxic and environmentally benign (no persistent fluorinated residues)
stable under typical processing temperatures (180–260 °C) and shear rates

final_candidate.justification: PDMS possesses a silicon–oxygen backbone that confers extremely low surface energy (~20 mN/m) and excellent lubricity, yielding COF values below 0.15 on many polymer substrates. The microcapsule formulation allows uniform dispersion in the melt without significant viscosity increase, preserving mechanical properties of the host thermoplastic. PDMS is stable up to ~300 °C, compatible with PET, PP, PE, PC, and ABS processing temperatures, and is non‑PFAS, meeting all chemical constraints.

process_recipe.description: Dry the base polymer resin (e.g., PET) at 80 °C for 2 h to remove moisture.
Pre‑mix PDMS microcapsules with the dried polymer in a high‑shear mixer to achieve uniform distribution.
process_recipe.conditions: Room temperature, 5 min mixing at 2000 rpm

process_recipe.description: Compound the mixture in a twin‑screw extruder to melt and blend the components.
process_recipe.conditions: Temperature profile: 240 °C (zone 1), 250 °C (zones 2–4); screw speed 100 rpm; residence time ~5 min

process_recipe.description: Pelletize the compounded material and cool to room temperature.
process_recipe.conditions: Cooling water bath, pelletizer speed 200 rpm

process_recipe.description: Injection mold test specimens or parts using the PDMS‑filled PET pellets.
process_recipe.conditions: Mold temperature 80 °C; melt temperature 260 °C; injection pressure 70 MPa
